phrases.intent_en: Ask what the Quadrilatero Romano is
phrases.cultural_note: The Quadrilatero Romano is Turin's oldest grid of streets, following the original Roman camp layout, now a buzzing area of restaurants and bars.

responses.english: It's Turin's oldest historic centre, dating to Roman times.
responses.phonetic: eh eel CHEN-troh STOH-ree-koh PYOO an-TEE-koh dee toh-REE-noh, EH-poh-kah roh-MAH-nah.
responses.french: È il centro storico più antico di Torino, epoca romana.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find the best restaurants in the Quadrilatero
phrases.text_local: Buongiorno, dove si mangia bene nel Quadrilatero?
phrases.pronunciation: bwohn-JOHR-noh, DOH-veh see MAN-jah BEH-neh nel kwah-dree-LAH-teh-roh?

responses.english: On Via Santa Chiara and Via della Consolata.
responses.phonetic: een VEE-ah SAN-tah KYAH-rah eh VEE-ah DEL-lah kon-soh-LAH-tah.
responses.french: In Via Santa Chiara e Via della Consolata.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get to the Quadrilatero from Piazza Castello
phrases.text_local: Scusi, come si raggiunge il Quadrilatero da Piazza Castello?
phrases.pronunciation: SKOO-zee, KOH-meh see raj-JOO-njeh eel kwah-dree-LAH-teh-roh dah PYAHT-tsah kas-TEL-loh?

responses.english: On foot, five minutes north along Via dell'Arsenale.
responses.phonetic: ah PYEH-dee, CHEEN-kweh mee-NOO-tee VER-soh nord per VEE-ah del-lar-seh-NAH-leh.
responses.french: A piedi, cinque minuti verso nord per Via dell'Arsenale.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the daily morning market is
phrases.text_local: Buongiorno, dove si fa il mercato mattutino qui?
phrases.pronunciation: bwohn-JOHR-noh, DOH-veh see fah eel mer-KAH-toh mat-too-TEE-noh kwee?

responses.english: At Piazza della Repubblica, Porta Palazzo, every morning.
responses.phonetic: een PYAHT-tsah DEL-lah reh-POOB-lee-kah, POR-tah pah-LAHT-tsoh, ON-nyee mat-TEE-nah.
responses.french: In Piazza della Repubblica, Porta Palazzo, ogni mattina.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Turin Shroud cathedral is
phrases.text_local: Scusi, dov'è il Duomo con la Sindone?
phrases.pronunciation: SKOO-zee, doh-VEH eel DWOH-moh kon lah SEEN-doh-neh?

responses.english: Two steps away, at Piazza San Giovanni.
responses.phonetic: ah DOO-eh PAS-see, een PYAHT-tsah san joh-VAN-nee.
responses.french: A due passi, in Piazza San Giovanni.
